title: penstemon stenophyllus
intro: Penstemon stenophyllus, also known as narrow-leaved beardtongue, is a perennial plant species in the family Plantaginaceae, native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and antitussive properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and salves, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
